cases.idea: A surveillance system that detects motion using video analysis and triggers alerts when unauthorized activity is identified. The system monitors a camera feed, compares frames or extracted visual features, and detects movement in protected areas. It can define restricted zones, ignore background noise, and notify security personnel when activity appears outside permitted times or locations. This is a standard video security and monitoring application.

full_result.user_idea: A surveillance system that detects motion using video analysis and triggers alerts when unauthorized activity is identified. The system monitors a camera feed, compares frames or extracted visual features, and detects movement in protected areas. It can define restricted zones, ignore background noise, and notify security personnel when activity appears outside permitted times or locations. This is a standard video security and monitoring application.

A surveillance system that detects motion using video analysis and triggers alerts when unauthorized activity is identified. This is a standard video security and monitoring application.

cases.idea: A predictive maintenance system that monitors machine vibrations and predicts failures using time-series analysis. Sensors collect vibration signals from industrial equipment, feature extraction identifies unusual frequency patterns, and a trained model estimates whether the machine is approaching a fault condition. Maintenance teams can then inspect or repair equipment before breakdowns occur. This type of condition-monitoring pipeline is widely used in manufacturing and industrial IoT.

This is an established AR interaction pattern used in headsets and mixed-reality systems.

cases.idea: An electronic health record system that stores patient data and allows secure sharing between healthcare providers. The system maintains medical history, diagnoses, medications, lab results, imaging reports, and clinical notes in a centralized or interoperable digital format. Access controls and audit logs are used to protect sensitive patient information. Such systems are standard infrastructure in hospitals, clinics, and large healthcare organizations.

cases.idea: A cashierless checkout system that uses computer vision to track items selected by customers and automatically charges their account. Cameras and shelf sensors observe which products are taken, returned, or carried out of the store. The system links detected item interactions to a customer session and generates a receipt after exit. This is an established retail automation concept used in checkout-free store prototypes and deployments.

cases.idea: A solar panel tracking system that incorporates short-range cloud cover forecasts from a sky-facing camera. Instead of only tracking the sun's position, the controller predicts temporary shading events and adjusts panel orientation before they occur. It can also park panels at an aerodynamically safer angle when shading coincides with high wind risk. The contribution is an adaptive tracking policy that combines solar optimization with local sky-image forecasting and mechanical load reduction.

full_result.user_idea: A solar panel tracking system that incorporates short-range cloud cover forecasts from a sky-facing camera. Instead of only tracking the sun's position, the controller predicts temporary shading events and adjusts panel orientation before they occur. It can also park panels at an aerodynamically safer angle when shading coincides with high wind risk. The contribution is an adaptive tracking policy that combines solar optimization with local sky-image forecasting and mechanical load reduction.
full_result.enriched_idea:
RESEARCH IDEA:
A solar panel tracking system that incorporates short-range cloud cover forecasts from a sky-facing camera. Instead of only tracking the sun's position, the controller predicts temporary shading events and adjusts panel orientation before they occur. It can also park panels at an aerodynamically safer angle when shading coincides with high wind risk. The contribution is an adaptive tracking policy that combines solar optimization with local sky-image forecasting and mechanical load reduction.

cases.idea: A surveillance system that combines skeleton pose estimation with spatial zone modeling to detect loitering, tailgating, and prohibited postures. Rather than storing identifiable facial imagery, the system extracts body keypoints and compares movement patterns against rules for protected areas. It can flag suspicious behavior while reducing reliance on facial recognition. This extends conventional motion-based surveillance with privacy-preserving pose-level behavioral analysis.

cases.idea: A predictive maintenance system that learns machine-specific degradation curves by fine-tuning a cross-factory foundation model on local sensor history. The base model captures general vibration, temperature, and acoustic failure patterns across many factories, while local adaptation accounts for equipment-specific wear behavior. This reduces cold-start error for newly installed machines with limited failure history. The idea extends standard predictive maintenance with transfer learning and local calibration.

RESEARCH IDEA:
A predictive maintenance system that learns machine-specific degradation curves by fine-tuning a cross-factory foundation model on local sensor history. The base model captures general vibration, temperature, and acoustic failure patterns across many factories, while local adaptation accounts for equipment-specific wear behavior. This reduces cold-start error for newly installed machines with limited failure history. The idea extends standard predictive maintenance with transfer learning and local calibration.

cases.idea: An electronic health record system that uses a fine-tuned clinical language model to generate structured problem-list summaries from unstructured physician notes. Extracted diagnoses, symptoms, medications, and follow-up items are linked back to their source sentences for verification. Clinicians can approve, edit, or reject the generated summary before it becomes part of the official record. This improves EHR usability while keeping human review in the workflow.

Clinicians can approve, edit, or reject the generated summary before it becomes part of the official record. This improves EHR usability while keeping human review in the workflow.

cases.idea: A cashierless checkout system that resolves item-identity ambiguity between visually similar products by fusing shelf weight sensor readings with overhead camera detections. When two products have similar packaging, the reconciliation layer compares visual evidence, shelf position, weight change, and customer hand trajectory. It assigns a probability to each candidate product before updating the virtual cart. This extends checkout-free retail systems with more robust multimodal disambiguation.

full_result.user_idea: A factory floor where machines form temporary production coalitions using signed capability tokens. Incoming work orders are not assigned by a scheduler; instead, machines negotiate task ownership based on their current capacity, tool state, and local commitments.

cases.idea: A clearing system where settlement timing changes dynamically according to each transaction's predicted contribution to systemic risk. cases.idea: A treatment planning system that represents disease progression as a directed influence graph over biological subsystems. It identifies interventions by computing the smallest set of biological control points needed to disconnect pathological activation cascades from organ damage nodes.

full_result.user_idea: A treatment planning system that represents disease progression as a directed influence graph over biological subsystems. It identifies interventions by computing the smallest set of biological control points needed to disconnect pathological activation cascades from organ damage nodes.
full_result.enriched_idea:
RESEARCH IDEA:
A treatment planning system that represents disease progression as a directed influence graph over biological subsystems. It identifies interventions by computing the smallest set of biological control points needed to disconnect pathological activation cascades from organ damage nodes.

cases.idea: A dynamic pricing system where each product is controlled by a decentralized shelf-level agent that observes only local interaction signals such as pick-ups, returns, dwell time, and abandonment. The agent adjusts price using no-regret online learning without direct access to store-wide inventory or competitor data. Nearby product agents indirectly influence each other only through customer behavior in shared physical space. This makes pricing emerge from local embodied retail interactions rather than centralized demand forecasting.

cases.idea: An identity verification system that represents a person as a time-varying stochastic process over behavioral signals such as gait, touch pressure, device handling, and interaction timing. Instead of authenticating through a fixed fingerprint or face template, the system tests whether a new observation sequence is likely to have been generated by the enrolled behavioral process. Authentication becomes a sequential likelihood decision that adapts as the user's behavior naturally changes. This reframes identity as a living temporal pattern rather than a static biometric snapshot.

Authentication becomes a sequential likelihood decision that adapts as the user's behavior naturally changes. This reframes identity as a living temporal pattern rather than a static biometric snapshot.

cases.idea: A cloud scheduler that models each tenant's workload as a neural marked temporal point process and pre-warms compute capacity along predicted burst trajectories. Instead of reserving fixed capacity or reacting only after traffic spikes, the scheduler estimates when, where, and what type of workload burst is likely to occur. It then stages temporary compute resources before the burst arrives and releases them if the predicted trajectory collapses. The goal is to reduce cold-start latency without persistent over-provisioning.

full_result.user_idea: A cloud scheduler that models each tenant's workload as a neural marked temporal point process and pre-warms compute capacity along predicted burst trajectories. Instead of reserving fixed capacity or reacting only after traffic spikes, the scheduler estimates when, where, and what type of workload burst is likely to occur. It then stages temporary compute resources before the burst arrives and releases them if the predicted trajectory collapses. The goal is to reduce cold-start latency without persistent over-provisioning.
full_result.enriched_idea:
RESEARCH IDEA:
A cloud scheduler that models each tenant's workload as a neural marked temporal point process and pre-warms compute capacity along predicted burst trajectories. Instead of reserving fixed capacity or reacting only after traffic spikes, the scheduler estimates when, where, and what type of workload burst is likely to occur. It then stages temporary compute resources before the burst arrives and releases them if the predicted trajectory collapses. The goal is to reduce cold-start latency without persistent over-provisioning.

cases.idea: A home automation system that constructs a causal model of resident behavior from passive sensor streams and uses counterfactual reasoning to infer unstated goals. Instead of merely learning repeated routines, the system asks what outcome the resident was likely trying to achieve under different device states. It then proposes new automation rules that would have produced the desired outcome with less effort. The resident must approve each inferred rule before activation, making the system a causal assistant rather than a purely pattern-based automation engine.

cases.idea: A computing interface that continuously estimates a user's cognitive load from typing cadence, cursor micro-movements, interaction latency, and task-switching rhythm. The workspace then restructures itself by collapsing, surfacing, or reordering interface elements according to available attentional bandwidth. Unlike static accessibility modes or manual focus settings, the interface changes in response to moment-by-moment cognitive state. The goal is to make the software environment contract under overload and expand again when the user can handle more information.

full_result.enriched_idea:
RESEARCH IDEA:
A computing interface that continuously estimates a user's cognitive load from typing cadence, cursor micro-movements, interaction latency, and task-switching rhythm. The workspace then restructures itself by collapsing, surfacing, or reordering interface elements according to available attentional bandwidth. Unlike static accessibility modes or manual focus settings, the interface changes in response to moment-by-moment cognitive state. The goal is to make the software environment contract under overload and expand again when the user can handle more information.
